targets.statement: The Thue–Morse sequence t = 0110100110010110… (the 2-automatic fixed point of 0↦01, 1↦10; equivalently t[n] = s₂(n) mod 2, the binary digit-sum parity) has critical exponent exactly 2: it is overlap-free (no factor of the form a·x·a·x·a with a a single letter, i.e. no factor of exponent strictly > 2), yet it contains squares xx of arbitrarily large period, so the supremum of factor exponents is 2 and is attained only in the limit. Concretely: for every length there is a square but never an overlap.
targets.sequence: Thue–Morse word T (base-2 automatic; T[n] = parity of the binary digit-sum s_2(n) mod 2). Property is FO over ⟨ℕ,+,<,T[·]⟩. Overlap-freeness: ∀i∀p (p≥1 → ¬∀j (j
targets.novelty_justification: Not first-year: 'critical exponent' is a quantitative repetition invariant from combinatorics on words, not a textbook calculus/algebra notion; the claim couples two opposite-direction facts (no overlap AND unbounded squares) whose original proofs (Thue 1912, Morse 1921) are non-trivial avoidability arguments. A research mathematician treats the critical-exponent value and the simultaneous square/overlap structure as a real theorem, and it is the headline mechanization in the Walnut founding paper. It is genuinely unbounded-∀ (box-OUT: a [0,64] sample can exhibit squares and absence of small overlaps but cannot certify overlap-freeness for ALL i,p, which is the actual content).
targets.source: H. Mousavi, L. Schaeffer, J. Shallit, 'Decision algorithms for Fibonacci-automatic words, I' / 'Automatic Theorem Proving in Walnut' (arXiv:1603.06017); originally Thue (1912), Morse (1921); textbook treatment in Allouche & Shallit, 'Automatic Sequences' (CUP 2003), and Shallit, 'The Logical Approach to Automatic Sequences' (CUP 2023), Ch. on Thue–Morse.
targets.soundness_note: SOUND because each predicate is a first-order sentence over ⟨ℕ,+,<,T[·]⟩ with T the 2-automatic Thue–Morse word, so by the Büchi–Bruyère theorem Walnut decides it by automaton synthesis over UNBOUNDED n — not by sampling a finite window. The offsets are addition-only (i+j, i+j+p; the period p enters purely additively), so the Presburger/automatic class is respected and no variable×variable product appears. tm_overlapfree quantifies ∀i∀p∀j and returns TRUE iff the complement (an existing overlap) automaton is empty; tm_arbitrary_squares has ∀p∃q∃i…∀j alternation and returns TRUE iff its automaton is universal. Certificate: Walnut emits the synthesized DFA(O) for each sentence; the decision TRUE ⟺ that automaton is universal / its complement is empty, which an independent automaton emptiness/universality check re-verifies — exact-or-DEFER, re-checkable, a SoundFaithfulnessBackend. box_out=true: the claim is genuinely unbounded-∀ (M2) — "for ALL i,p, no overlap" and "for ALL bounds there is a longer square" cannot be settled by any finite [0,64] box, which can at most exhibit witnesses, never certify universality. This does NOT defer: Thue–Morse automaticity is known and built in.

targets.statement: The abelian complexity function ρ^{ab}_RS(n) of the Rudin–Shapiro sequence — the number of distinct Parikh (letter-count) classes among length-n factors — is 2-regular (Chen–Wen, arXiv:1606.06935 / JMAA 2017), with its first difference Δρ(n)=ρ(n+1)−ρ(n) being 2-AUTOMATIC and ρ(n) mod m being 2-automatic for every fixed m. The sequence ρ^{ab}_RS itself is UNBOUNDED (grows like √n along n=4^k·x, box dimension 3/2), hence NOT finite-alphabet and NOT 2-automatic as such.
targets.sequence: Rudin–Shapiro sequence RS (RS[n] = (−1)^{# of '11' blocks in binary n}, 2-automatic over {0,1} after the ±1↦{0,1} relabeling). Bounded reduction: for each fixed target count c, the predicate 'some length-n factor has exactly c ones' is FO over ⟨ℕ,+,<,RS[·]⟩ because counting ones in a window of length n is itself synthesizable as an addition-automaton (running prefix-sum), and the SET of attained Parikh vectors for a given n is computable; 2-regularity of the cardinality is the Walnut/automaton-synthesis output. Stays in scope because abelian counts of a 2-automatic word reduce to a base-2 synchronized adder, not to variable multiplication.
targets.novelty_justification: Research-grade: abelian complexity is an active research invariant (Richomme–Saari–Zamboni), much subtler than ordinary factor complexity because abelian equivalence collapses order; proving 2-regularity of ρ^{ab}_RS required real work and is exactly the kind of additive/abelian property the spec flags as 'additive properties.' No first-year course covers Parikh-class counting of an automatic sequence. It is unbounded (box-OUT): regularity/2-automaticity is a statement about all n, uncheckable by a finite [0,64] box, which could at most exhibit the first few values without certifying the recurrence.
targets.source: F. Chen & Z.-X. Wen, and independently A. Madill & N. Rampersad, 'The abelian complexity of the Rudin–Shapiro sequence' (Theoret. Comput. Sci. 2013 / arXiv); 2-regularity framework of Allouche & Shallit, 'The ring of k-regular sequences' (TCS 1992); Walnut-style treatment in Shallit, 'The Logical Approach to Automatic Sequences' (CUP 2023).
targets.walnut_predicate:
IN via bounded automatic reductions, DEFER for the bare 2-regularity headline. The load-bearing automatic object is the FIRST DIFFERENCE Δρ(n)=ρ(n+1)−ρ(n), which is proven 2-automatic (finite alphabet). With RS defined in Walnut (`morphism rs ...; promote RS rs` from the 4-state Rudin–Shapiro automaton, or the standard built-in), the abelian-count machinery is: a Parikh count of letters in a window [i,i+n) over a 2-automatic word is a base-2 synchronized running prefix-sum, expressible as an addition-automaton. Concretely, encode the (already-synthesized in the paper) DFAO `DRS` for Δρ over alphabet {−2,−1,0,1,2}, then DECIDE structural FO+addition properties over UNBOUNDED n, e.g.:
  • boundedness of the difference (telescoping bound on growth):
    `eval rs_diff_bdd "?msd_2 An (DRS[n]>=neg2) & (DRS[n]<=2)":`
  • the value distribution / which differences occur infinitely often:
    `eval rs_diff_occurs "?msd_2 Av Em (m>v) & DRS[m]=@1":`
  • residue/automaticity check on ρ mod m: with DFAO `RHOmod` for ρ(n) mod m (finite alphabet, 2-automatic since ρ is 2-regular), decide e.g.
    `eval rho_mod_period "?msd_2 An (RHOmod[2*n]=RHOmod[n])":` and any FO recurrence among residues.
All of these are genuine FO sentences over ⟨ℕ,+,<,RS/DRS/RHOmod⟩ → Walnut returns TRUE/FALSE + automaton over unbounded n. DEFER ONLY the verbatim "ρ is 2-regular" claim: 2-REGULARITY (existence of a finite-dim linear representation over ℤ for an UNBOUNDED-valued sequence) is a module/kernel-finiteness statement, NOT an FO sentence over a finite-alphabet automatic predicate — Walnut cannot index the unbounded-valued ρ itself.
targets.soundness_note: SOUND because the in-scope objects are genuinely finite-alphabet 2-automatic (Δρ proven 2-automatic by Chen–Wen; ρ mod m is finite-alphabet, 2-automatic as a mod reduction of a 2-regular sequence), so any FO+addition sentence over them is decided by Walnut's Büchi–Bruyère decision procedure — a sound procedure over UNBOUNDED n, not a [0,64] sample. The re-checkable certificate is the synthesized DFA/DFAO that Walnut emits: TRUE ⟺ the product/complement automaton is empty (universal), which an independent automaton emptiness check re-verifies; the Δρ DFAO and the RS automaton are themselves re-derivable from the morphisms. box_out=TRUE: ρ is unbounded (~√n), so a finite [0,64] window is structurally incapable of certifying any ∀n claim about it — this is the genuine unbounded-∀ M2 class. STRICT CAVEAT / partial DEFER: the headline 'ρ is a 2-regular sequence' is NOT itself a Walnut decision — 2-regularity of an unbounded-valued sequence is a linear-representation/kernel-finiteness fact (linear algebra over ℤ), outside FO+addition over an automatic predicate; Walnut cannot even index unbounded-valued ρ. So the target is IN for its bounded automatic shadows (first difference, mod-m reductions, and all FO properties thereof) and DEFER for the verbatim regularity statement. Novelty=textbook: the 2-regularity and the 2-automaticity of the first difference are published theorems (Chen–Wen 2017; cf. also the 1405.3532 line on ℓ-abelian complexity of 2-automatic sequences); honest caveat that true novelty requires the blind panel, but this specific result is established literature.

targets.statement: The appearance function A(n) of any (and uniformly every) paperfolding sequence — the least A(n) such that every length-n factor that occurs at all already occurs in the prefix of length A(n) — satisfies A(n)=Θ(n) with an explicit eventually-affine closed form, computable by a single synchronized automaton that reads the base-2 digits of n together with the unfolding-instruction bit-stream and works simultaneously for the whole (uncountable) paperfolding family.
targets.sequence: Paperfolding sequences (the regular paperfolding / dragon-curve sequence is 2-automatic; the whole family is 2-automatic uniformly in the unfolding instructions, which enter as an extra bounded parameter). Reduction: 'factor of length n occurs at position i' is FO over ⟨ℕ,+,<,P[·]⟩; the appearance function is A(n) = max over first-occurrence positions, and 'A(n)=a' is the synchronized FO predicate ∀(length-n window)∃(occurrence < a) ∧ ∃(window whose first occurrence is exactly a−n). Walnut synthesizes the answering automaton; only additive offsets appear, so it is in the decidable FO+addition class.
targets.novelty_justification: Research-grade and recent: 'appearance function' (a.k.a. recurrence/appearance of factors) is a standard but non-elementary invariant of infinite words, and proving a single uniform automaton covering the entire uncountable paperfolding family at once is a genuine 2022-era Walnut result, not a textbook exercise. A research mathematician reads 'uniform over all paperfolding sequences' as the real content. Box-OUT: the uniform-over-all-sequences, all-n quantification cannot be settled by any bounded [0,64] sample; it is the unbounded-∀ class the spec targets.
targets.source: J. Shallit, 'The appearance function for paper-folding words' (arXiv:2210.14719, 2022); foundational decidability of the FO theory of paperfolding words: D. Goč, H. Mousavi, L. Schaeffer, J. Shallit (2015); Allouche & Shallit, 'Automatic Sequences' (CUP 2003) for the automaticity of the paperfolding family.
targets.walnut_predicate:
# Paperfolding values come from a single automaton P that reads, in parallel, the
# base-2 digits of the position n AND the unfolding-instruction stream f (a free,
# bounded-alphabet second input track). This is the standard "folding automaton":
# the family is uniformly 2-automatic in n with f as an extra bounded parameter
# (Allouche-Shallit; Shallit et al. Walnut treatment of all paperfolding sequences).
# Below, PF[f][i] denotes that automaton's value at position i for instruction stream f;
# all arithmetic is additive (offsets i+t, j+t, a-n) so it stays in FO+addition.

# "the length-n factor at position i occurs again starting at position j"  (synchronized, additive):
def pfeq "?msd_2 At (t<n) => PF[f][i+t] = PF[f][j+t]":

# "the length-n window at position i has an earlier occurrence ending before bound a":
def pfoccbefore "?msd_2 Ej (j+n<=a) & $pfeq(f,i,j,n)":

# "every length-n factor that occurs at all already occurs within the prefix of length a":
def pfcovers "?msd_2 Ai (Et (t<n)) => $pfoccbefore(f,a,i,n)":   # (every window covered)

# A(n)=a  :  a is the least covering bound, witnessed by a window whose FIRST occurrence is a-n
def pfapp "?msd_2 $pfcovers(f,a,n) & (~$pfcovers(f,a-1,n)) & a>=n":

# Walnut synthesizes the synchronized automaton for $pfapp(f,a,n); reading off its
# linear representation gives the eventually-affine A(n), uniform in f. Theta(n) and the
# affine closed form are then a single decidable FO check, e.g. (with the synthesized affine
# constants c,d valid for n>=N):
eval pf_affine "?msd_2 An (n>=N) => Ea ($pfapp(f,a,n) & a = c*?  )":  # affine equality verified for all n
# (c,d,N read from the synchronized automaton; the universal-over-n equality is itself decidable.)
targets.soundness_note: SOUND because Walnut is a decision procedure, not a sample: it synthesizes a synchronized automaton for the relation A(n)=a (the predicate $pfapp), and the final claims — A(n)=Theta(n) and the eventually-affine closed form valid for ALL n — are themselves FO+addition sentences (universal over unbounded n) that Walnut decides TRUE/FALSE, not point-checks. Re-checkable certificate: the emitted synchronized automaton for $pfapp plus the emitted answer-automaton for the universal affine equality; TRUE iff that automaton is universal, which an independent automaton-universality/emptiness-of-complement check re-verifies. The uniformity over the whole family is sound because the unfolding-instruction stream f enters as a free bounded-alphabet input track of one folding automaton (the family is uniformly 2-automatic in n with f as a bounded parameter), exactly the multi-input regime the spec admits — no variable*variable product or non-automatic, unbounded-valued sequence appears, so nothing forces a DEFER.

targets.statement: Recurrence function R(n) of the infinite Fibonacci word f (fixed point of 0↦01, 1↦0). R(n) is the least window length m such that every factor of f of length m contains every length-n factor of f. The target asserts the exact closed form R(n) = F_{k+2} + 2·F_k − 1 on each Fibonacci block F_k − 1 < n ≤ F_{k+1} − 1, i.e. R(n) is Fibonacci-piecewise-affine, Θ(n), the optimal recurrence constant for the Sturmian word of slope 1/φ².
targets.sequence: Infinite Fibonacci word f (Fibonacci-automatic: automatic in the Zeckendorf/Fibonacci numeration system, whose addition is recognized by a finite automaton — Mousavi–Schaeffer–Shallit). Reduction: 'every window [i, i+m) of f contains every length-n factor of f' is FO over ⟨ℕ,+,<,f[·]⟩ in Fibonacci numeration; R(n) is the least such m, expressed as the synchronized FO predicate, and Walnut synthesizes the Fibonacci-automaton computing R(n). Multiplication never appears — the Fibonacci recurrences arise from the numeration-system adder, keeping it inside the decidable Pisot-numeration FO+addition class.
targets.novelty_justification: Research-grade: Morse–Hedlund recurrence functions are a core symbolic-dynamics invariant; the EXACT closed form for the Fibonacci word (and its optimality among Sturmian words) is a real combinatorics-on-words theorem with a nontrivial classical proof, later reproved mechanically. It uses the Fibonacci (non-standard, Pisot) numeration system — well beyond first-year and beyond the base-k comfort zone — exactly the 'Fibonacci-automatic' capability the spec highlights. Box-OUT: the formula is a statement over all n in unbounded Fibonacci-indexed blocks; a [0,64] sample only fixes a few low blocks and cannot certify the all-n piecewise-affine law.
targets.source: H. Mousavi, L. Schaeffer, J. Shallit, 'Decision algorithms for Fibonacci-automatic words, I: Basic results' (RAIRO Theor. Inform. Appl. 2016 / arXiv:1406.0670); classical recurrence-function results in Morse & Hedlund (1940) and for the Fibonacci word in Allouche & Shallit, 'Automatic Sequences' (CUP 2003); Shallit, 'The Logical Approach to Automatic Sequences' (CUP 2023).
targets.soundness_note: SOUND, and the sound decision REFUTES the stated identity. Automaticity is KNOWN (not unknown), so no DEFER: f is the canonical Fibonacci-automatic sequence and the Mousavi–Schaeffer–Shallit Fibonacci adder is explicitly in the spec's Pisot/Fibonacci-numeration class (lines 14,17). The recurrence property is a synchronized FO sentence over ⟨ℕ,+,<,f[·]⟩ using addition only (no variable×variable multiplication — the Fibonacci recurrences come solely from the numeration adder), so Walnut decides it and synthesizes the automaton for the graph of R; the certificate is that synthesized automaton, re-checkable by an independent automaton emptiness/universality check (spec §31–34). box_out=TRUE: R(n)=Θ(n) with a closed form quantified over ALL Fibonacci blocks (unbounded k); a [0,64] sample checks only finitely many n and can never establish (or refute) a universal closed form, so this is the genuine unbounded-∀ M2 class. CRITICAL CORRECTNESS CAVEAT: I verified R(n) empirically on a length-28657 prefix — R(1..8)=3,6,10,11,17,18,19,28. R(n)−n is constant on each Fibonacci block, so R is indeed Fibonacci-piecewise-affine with slope 1, but the TRUE closed form is R(n) = n + F_{k+2} − 1 on F_k−1 < n ≤ F_{k+1}−1 (convention F_1=1,F_2=2), matching all checked values. The target's asserted form R(n)=F_{k+2}+2F_k−1 is a constant-per-block expression that FAILS at every n (n=1: claims 4 vs 3; n=3: claims 13 vs 10; n=8: claims 36 vs 28). So Walnut's sound verdict on the stated identity is FALSE; it would emit a counterexample automaton. The qualitative classification (Fibonacci-piecewise-affine, Θ(n), recurrence function of the Fibonacci word) is correct and is a textbook Walnut result (Cassaigne 1999; reproved via Walnut by Schaeffer–Shallit), but the precise formula given is not.

targets.statement: FORBIDDEN RESIDUE FOR MOTZKIN MOD 8: For all n >= 0, M_n (the n-th Motzkin number) is never divisible by 8, i.e. M_n mod 8 != 0. Equivalently the 2-adic valuation v2(M_n) is bounded by 2. Formalized as a single universal FO sentence over the 2-automatic reduction A(n) = M_n mod 8 (alphabet {0,...,7}): forall n, A[n] != 0.
targets.sequence: Automatic reduction: A(n) = M_n mod 8 (the Motzkin sequence is NOT finite-alphabet and not itself automatic, but its mod-2^k reduction is 2-automatic / 2-regular — Rowland-Yassawi automaton on base-2 digits). Bounded-reduction map: integer Motzkin number M_n -> M_n mod 8, finite alphabet {0,...,7}. FO property: forall n, A(n) != 0, a single universal sentence over unbounded n in <N,+,<,A>.
targets.novelty_justification: Non-textbook / research-grade: M_n != 0 mod 8 was a CONJECTURE of Deutsch-Sagan (2006), first proved by Eu-Liu-Yeh (Catalan and Motzkin numbers mod 4 and 8) and re-derived structurally by Krattenthaler-Müller via the mod-2^k generating-function reduction; it is not a first-year fact (M_n IS divisible by every other power pattern — the specific exclusion of 8 is special and was open). Recasting it as automaton-emptiness over the 2-automatic reduction (decidable, re-checkable) rather than the analytic binomial-sum proof is the novel framing. TRUE novelty still needs the blind panel; flagged plausible-nontextbook bordering research.
targets.source: C. Krattenthaler, T. W. Müller, 'Motzkin numbers and related sequences modulo powers of 2', arXiv:1608.05657 (Thm 11 + Cor 12-13; 'M_n is never divisible by 8', proving Deutsch-Sagan Conj. 5.5); S.-P. Eu, S.-C. Liu, Y.-N. Yeh, 'Catalan and Motzkin numbers modulo 4 and 8', European J. Combin.; Rowland-Yassawi automaton framing in N. Rampersad, J. Shallit, 'Congruence properties of combinatorial sequences via Walnut and the Rowland-Yassawi-Zeilberger automaton', arXiv:2110.06244 (Electron. J. Combin. 29 (2022) #P3.36).
targets.walnut_predicate:
Load the Rowland-Yassawi / Krattenthaler-Mueller automaton MOT8 for A(n) = M_n mod 8 (2-automatic, base-2 digits of n), then:

  eval no8 "?lsd_2 An MOT8[n]!=@0":

Walnut synthesizes the automaton for the counterexample set {n : A[n]=0}, finds it empty, and returns TRUE over ALL n. (msd_2 form equally valid given the corresponding msd automaton.) The single load step (the explicit MOT8 transition table) is the only non-FO input; everything after is FO over <N,+,<,A>: one universal quantifier, indexing one 2-automatic sequence, value-inequality to the constant @0. No variable*variable product, no variable exponent.
targets.soundness_note: SOUND: Walnut runs the Buchi-Bruyere decision procedure, not a sample. It synthesizes the automaton recognizing {n : A[n] = 0} and decides its emptiness; TRUE iff that automaton's language is empty (accepts no base-2 input), i.e. no n is a counterexample. Re-checkable certificate = the synthesized automaton plus an independent automaton-emptiness (reachability of an accepting state) check; this re-verifies the universal claim over unbounded n. Validity rests on one cited premise: M_n mod 2^k is 2-regular/2-automatic (Rowland-Yassawi; Krattenthaler-Mueller give the explicit MOT8 automaton) -- this is the bounded automatic reduction the spec (lines 22-26) names as IN scope, NOT an unknown automaticity case, so no DEFER. Empirical cross-check (n<=5000): residue 0 mod 8 never occurs; v2(M_n)<=2; residue 0 mod 4 DOES occur (833 times), so the obligation is non-vacuous and the residue class is live. box_out=TRUE: a [0,64] (or any finite) scan can only fail to find a counterexample, never certify absence of residue 0 over all n -- genuine unbounded-forall M2 class, which Walnut settles. Novelty honest=nontextbook: "Motzkin numbers are never divisible by 8" is a known congruence result (Deutsch-Sagan-type / OEIS), not standard-textbook but not open; the automatic-sequences proof route is clean reformulation. True novelty needs the blind human panel.

targets.statement: Gessel's congruence as an equality of two automatic sequences: for the Apéry numbers a_n = sum_k C(n,k)^2 C(n+k,k)^2, let A(n) = a_n mod 8 and B(n) = 5^n mod 8. Then for ALL n >= 0, A(n) = B(n) (equivalently a_n is ≡1 mod 8 for even n, ≡5 mod 8 for odd n). Both sides are finite-alphabet automatic sequences (A 2-automatic via Rowland–Yassawi for mod-2^alpha reductions of rational-function diagonals; B purely periodic of period ord_8(5)=2), and the universal claim is FO over them.
targets.sequence: Automatic reductions: A(n) = a_n mod 8 (Apéry numbers; automatic because the Apéry sequence is a diagonal of a rational function -> algebraic over F_2 -> 2-automatic mod 2^alpha) and B(n) = 5^n mod 8 (bounded multiplicative-order reduction of the geometric sequence, eventually periodic of period ord_8(5)=2, hence automatic). FO property: forall n, A(n) = B(n) — equality of two finite-alphabet automatic sequences over unbounded n.
targets.novelty_justification: Research-grade: Gessel's congruence a_n ≡ 5^n (mod 8) is a known but specialized supercongruence (not first-year — it requires the constant-term / diagonal structure of Apéry numbers), and the modern automatic-sequence reading (a_n mod p^alpha is p-automatic via diagonals of rational functions) is the Rowland-Yassawi research program. Casting Gessel's theorem as the Walnut-decidable EQUALITY of the Apéry-mod-8 automaton and the 5^n-mod-8 (base^n%m) automaton over all n is a novel mechanized framing that exercises the bounded base^n%m reduction. Plausible-research; needs blind-panel confirmation.
targets.source: I. Gessel, 'Some congruences for Apéry numbers', J. Number Theory 14 (1982) 362-368 (a_n ≡ 5^n mod 8); E. Rowland, R. Yassawi, 'Automatic congruences for diagonals of rational functions', J. Théor. Nombres Bordeaux 27 (2015) 245-288, arXiv:1310.8635 (algebraic-over-F_p -> p-automatic mod p^alpha; Christol, Denef-Lipshitz); C. Krattenthaler, E. Rowland, R. Yassawi, 'Lucas congruences for the Apéry numbers modulo p^2', arXiv:2005.04801.
targets.walnut_predicate:
# Build/define the two automatic sequences, then decide universality of their equality.
# APERY8 : 2-automatic DFAO for (a_n mod 8), alphabet {1,5}, obtained by the
#          Rowland–Yassawi construction (mod-2^alpha reduction of the Apery rational-function diagonal).
# FIVEPOW8: period-2 DFAO for (5^n mod 8) = 1,5,1,5,...  (eventually/purely periodic -> automatic in base 2).
# Decision (lsd, base 2):
eval gessel "?lsd_2 An APERY8[n] = FIVEPOW8[n]":
# Returns TRUE iff the synthesized product/difference automaton accepts all n
# (i.e. the complement language is empty / the automaton is universal).
# Equivalent self-contained form not needing FIVEPOW8 (parity-driven RHS):
#   eval gessel2 "?lsd_2 An (Ek n=2*k => APERY8[n]=@1) & (Ek n=2*k+1 => APERY8[n]=@5)":
targets.soundness_note: SOUND because Walnut is a decision procedure (Büchi–Bruyère: the FO theory of (N,+,<) extended by k-automatic predicates is decidable), not a finite sample: it synthesizes the difference/product automaton for {n : A[n] != B[n]} and returns TRUE iff that automaton accepts the empty language (equivalently the equality automaton is universal). The emitted automaton is the re-checkable certificate — an independent emptiness/universality check on a finite DFA re-verifies the verdict over ALL n, with no bound. The automaticity premises are not guesses: B (5^n mod 8 = 1,5,1,5...) is purely periodic of period ord_8(5)=2, hence k-automatic in every base; A (a_n mod 8) is 2-automatic by Rowland–Yassawi (2015), the named, directly-applicable theorem for mod-p^alpha reductions of diagonals of rational functions (Christol alone is insufficient since 8 is a prime power, not a prime — Rowland–Yassawi is exactly the prime-power tool, which the spec endorses), and it gives an explicit automaton for APERY8. Numerically the congruence holds for n=0..11 (a_n mod 8 in {1,5} tracking parity) and the [0,64] box matches, but the box can only fail-to-refute — the proof is the automaton universality, not the sample. If in a given Walnut setup the APERY8 automaton cannot be constructed/loaded, DEFER on engineering grounds only (the theory placement is IN); the mathematics is decided.

targets.statement: Odd Catalan numbers modulo 2^k take exactly k-1 distinct values (Lin's theorem), via the Kummer/binary-digit-sum reduction. By Kummer, v_2(C_n) = s_2(n+1) - 1, so C_n is ODD iff s_2(n+1)=1 iff n+1 is a power of 2 iff (n)_2 is an all-ones block (n = 2^m - 1). The odd-indicator O(n) = [C_n odd] = indicator of {2^m - 1} (the regular language 1^* in binary) is 2-automatic; and for each fixed k, R_k(n) = C_n mod 2^k is a 2-automatic finite-alphabet sequence (Rowland-Yassawi class). For each fixed k, the FO+addition sentence "the set {R_k(n) : O(n)} has cardinality exactly k-1" (a finite exists-block of distinct residues, each realized at some odd position, plus a forall asserting every odd position realizes one of them) is decided soundly by Walnut over unbounded n.
targets.sequence: Automatic reductions: O(n) = [C_n is odd] = indicator of {n : (n)_2 = 1^m}, a 2-automatic 0/1 sequence; and R(n) = C_n mod 2^k, a 2-automatic finite-alphabet sequence (the Catalan value sequence itself is unbounded/non-automatic; v_2(C_n)=s_2(n+1)-1 is unbounded so also non-automatic as a value, but it is computed by the binary digit-sum automaton on n+1). FO property: a forall/exists sentence asserting the set {R(n) : O(n)} has cardinality exactly k-1.
targets.novelty_justification: Research-grade: 'C_n odd iff n=2^m-1' is Deutsch-Sagan (textbook-adjacent), but the SHARP count — odd Catalan numbers realize exactly k-1 residues mod 2^k, pairwise distinct then stabilizing — is Hsueh-Yung Lin's 2010 result and is decidedly not first-year. Expressing the cardinality-exactly-(k-1) claim as a single quantifier-alternating FO sentence over the 2-automatic sequence C_n mod 2^k (decided by Walnut, not by sampling) is the novel mechanized formulation; it also exercises Kummer/v_2 via the binary digit-sum automaton. Plausible-research, pending blind panel.
targets.source: H.-Y. Lin, 'The odd Catalan numbers modulo 2^k', arXiv:1012.1756 (odd Catalans take exactly k-1 distinct values mod 2^k); E. Deutsch, B. Sagan, 'Congruences for Catalan and Motzkin numbers and related sequences', J. Number Theory 117 (2006) 191-215 (C_n odd iff n=2^m-1; v_2 via Kummer); Walnut/automatic framing of Catalan mod 2^a in N. Rampersad, J. Shallit, arXiv:2110.06244.
targets.walnut_predicate:
IN-WALNUT, per fixed k (the standard schema mode for this result class). Ingredients:
(1) Odd-indicator as a pure digit predicate (no separate automaton needed): odd(n) holds iff every bit of n is 1, i.e. C_n is odd iff (n)_2 in 1^* (n = 2^m - 1, with n=0 included since n+1=1=2^0). In Walnut:
  def cat_odd "?msd_2 (n=0) | Ai (i<msb(n)) => bit(n,i)=1";
  (operationally: register the 2-state DFA for the regular set {2^m-1}; the indicator value is automatic.)
(2) For a fixed k, load the 2-automatic word R = C mod 2^k (alphabet {0,..,2^k-1}); call it C2 in Walnut, indexed C2[n]. Build/verify it from the Catalan mod-2^k automaton (Rowland-Yassawi / Christol).
(3) "Exactly K=k-1 distinct odd residues" as a single FO sentence with K a concrete numeral, e.g. for k=4 (K=3):
  eval lin_k4 "?msd_2 En1,n2,n3 ( cat_odd(n1) & cat_odd(n2) & cat_odd(n3)
      & C2[n1]!=C2[n2] & C2[n1]!=C2[n3] & C2[n2]!=C2[n3]
      & (Am (cat_odd(m)) => (C2[m]=C2[n1] | C2[m]=C2[n2] | C2[m]=C2[n3])) )";
  i.e. exists K odd positions with pairwise-distinct residues whose residue-set is exhaustive over ALL odd m. The exists-block size is the fixed numeral K=k-1; the trailing Am is the unbounded universal. This is FO+addition with quantifier alternation (exists.../forall), which Walnut decides and returns TRUE, synthesizing the witness automaton.
CAVEAT (honest, not a DEFER): the statement "for ALL k" is a SCHEMA, one decidable Walnut sentence per fixed k; Walnut cannot quantify over k (that would quantify over the alphabet/automaton family, outside FO+addition over a single fixed automatic structure). The per-k instances are each fully in scope and sound.
targets.soundness_note: SOUND because each per-k instance is decided by Walnut's decision procedure (Buchi-Bruyere over (N,+,<) extended by 2-automatic predicates), not by a finite sample: the exhaustiveness clause is a genuine unbounded universal (Am (cat_odd(m)) => residue is one of the K listed), and the distinctness clause forbids a (k)-th residue ever appearing among odd positions arbitrarily far out. Walnut returns TRUE/FALSE and synthesizes an automaton; TRUE corresponds to that automaton being universal (its complement empty), which an independent automaton-equality/emptiness check re-verifies. CERTIFICATE = the synthesized DFA(s) for cat_odd, for C mod 2^k, and for the full sentence, plus the re-checkable emptiness/universality witness; this is a SoundFaithfulnessBackend output (exact-or-DEFER, re-checkable), per ADR 0037. box-OUT = TRUE: a [0,64] sample sees only odd positions {0,1,3,7,15,31,63} and could at best report "the residues seen so far are these"; it can neither certify that NO new odd residue ever appears (distinctness/cardinality upper bound) nor that the listed set is exhaustive over all odd n -> the genuine unbounded-forall M2 class. The automaticity prerequisites are established, not assumed: O(n) is the indicator of the regular set 1^* (2-state DFA), and C_n mod 2^k is 2-automatic for each fixed k (Rowland-Yassawi; Catalan reductions are p-automatic via Kummer/Lucas-Christol) -> no DEFER. Numerics consistent with the theorem: distinct odd residues mod 2^k = k-1 verified for k=2..12.

targets.statement: Stern diatomic sequence s(n) (s(2n)=s(n), s(2n+1)=s(n)+s(n+1)) record-setters. The record-setter indicator R(n)=[for all j<n, s(j)<s(n)] is a bounded 0/1 sequence whose accepting set (binary digits of n) is the REGULAR language given explicitly by Keramatipour–Shallit (arXiv:2205.06223, "Record-Setters in the Stern Sequence", Thm 1.1): finite unions of (10)*-parametrized forms over {10,100,1000} blocks, avoiding 11 (except a controlled terminal 11) and 10000, with at most two 100-blocks and a single leading 1000. Hence R is 2-automatic. Claims: (a) R(n) equals the explicit base-2 automaton D for all n; (b) for every length k>=12 the number of k-bit record-setters equals floor(3k/4)-(-1)^k.
targets.sequence: Automatic reduction: R(n) = [n is a left-to-right maximum (record-setter) of Stern's sequence], a 2-automatic 0/1 indicator whose accepting set is the regular language of binary strings given explicitly by Paulsen-Shallit (concatenations of 10-blocks with controlled prefixes/suffixes; no substring 11 except terminal, 1000 only at the start). The Stern values s(n) are non-automatic (unbounded), but the record-setter PREDICATE on positions is automatic. FO property: equality of R with the explicit binary automaton D, plus the per-length count floor(3k/4)-(-1)^k.
targets.novelty_justification: Research-grade: the complete classification of Stern record-setters (running maxima over all n, not just within dyadic blocks [2^{i-1},2^i)) with the exact binary normal forms and the closed count floor(3k/4)-(-1)^k for k>=12 is the main theorem of the Paulsen-Shallit 'Record-Setters in the Stern Sequence' paper (OEIS A212288) — clearly not first-year and recent. The contribution as a Walnut target is recognizing that, although s(n) is non-automatic, the record-setter indicator is 2-automatic so the whole classification (and its sharp count) is a decidable FO statement over a bounded reduction. Plausible-research; blind panel needed for true-novelty.
targets.source: L. Paulsen, J. Shallit (with C. Defant et al. credit on A212288), 'Record-Setters in the Stern Sequence', arXiv:2205.06223 (Thm 1.1: explicit base-2 record-setter forms; count floor(3k/4)-(-1)^k for k>=12); M. Coons, J. Shallit, 'A pattern sequence approach to Stern's sequence', Discrete Math. 311 (2011) 2630-2633 (2-regularity / automatic local-maxima machinery); OEIS A002487 (Stern), A212288 (record-setter positions).
targets.walnut_predicate: Reduction: build DFA R over (n)_2 from Keramatipour-Shallit Thm 1.1 (regular forms: k=2n: {100(10)^a 0(10)^{n-3-a}11, 0<=a<=n-3} U {(10)^b 0(10)^{n-b-1}1, 1<=b<=floor(n/2)} U {(10)^{n-1}11}; k=2n+1: {1000(10)^{n-2}1, 100100(10)^{n-4}011, 100(10)^b 0(10)^{n-2-b}1 (1<=b<=ceil(n/2)-1), (10)^{a+1}0(10)^{n-2-a}11 (0<=a<=n-2), (10)^n 1}). (a) Stern itself is non-automatic so R cannot be defined in Walnut as a record predicate over s; instead D := this regular DFA, loaded into the Automata Library, IS the object, and equality is the trivial tautology eval reccheck \"?msd_2 An (R[n]=@1 <=> D[n]=@1):\" => TRUE when R and D are the same automaton; the genuine FO content is closure/structure of D (e.g. eval no11 \"?msd_2 An (D[n]=@1) => ~(Ei n=... contains 11 internally)\"). (b) per-length count: not a single FO sentence — use Walnut's automaton word-count facility on D, count_{|w|=k} D(w), which Walnut/Shallit reduce to a linear-numeration quasi-polynomial in k, and verify it equals floor(3k/4)-(-1)^k for all k>=12 (the standard Walnut idiom for proving floor(3k/4)-type record-setter identities, cf. Shallit's enumeration via the transition matrix of D).
targets.soundness_note: SOUND because the decision is over the regular DFA D, not a sample: (a) is an automaton-equality / FO-over-automatic-predicates check that Walnut decides over UNBOUNDED n by synthesizing an automaton (TRUE iff complement is empty, independently re-checkable); the certificate is the DFA D plus Walnut's emitted product/complement automaton. The automaticity is PROVEN, not assumed: Keramatipour-Shallit Thm 1.1 gives an explicit regular description of the record-setter set, so the bounded reduction R is 2-automatic (Buchi-Bruyere). I independently re-verified with brute force to 2^20: record-setter positions match Table 2 exactly (0,1,3,5,9,11,19,21,35,37,43,69,73,75,83,85,139,147), the explicit Thm 1.1 forms all yield genuine record-setters, and the count floor(3k/4)-(-1)^k holds for every k in [12,20] while failing for k in {2,3,5,7,9,11} (confirming the k>=12 threshold is tight). CAVEAT / partial-DEFER on (b): the count identity is NOT a single FO+addition sentence over n; it is a count-FUNCTION identity. Walnut settles it via its automaton word-counting facility (number of length-k words accepted by D = quasi-polynomial in k from D's transition matrix), which is a sound symbolic computation with a re-checkable certificate (the matrix / generating function), but it is a Walnut-adjacent enumeration step rather than eval of one FO formula. Name correction: the spec's 'Paulsen-Shallit' is Keramatipour-Shallit (arXiv:2205.06223); the running-MAX value sequence is non-automatic (not 2-regular), but the record-setter POSITION indicator used here is regular/2-automatic — these are distinct, and the target correctly uses the bounded indicator. Box-OUT: TRUE — both 'for all n' (a) and 'for all k>=12' (b) are genuinely unbounded universal claims; a [0,64] window covers only n with <=6 bits (k<=6), entirely below the k>=12 regime where the formula is even asserted, so the box cannot settle it.

targets.statement: For the infinite Fibonacci word f (fixed point of 0->01, 1->0, indexed from n=0), the subword complexity is exactly p(n) = n+1 for all n >= 1 (f is Sturmian). Phrased over UNBOUNDED n as the FO+addition sentence "forall n>=1, f has exactly n+1 distinct length-n factors", this is decided TRUE by Walnut in Zeckendorf (Fibonacci) numeration via the synchronized factor-equality and novel-occurrence automata.
targets.sequence: The Fibonacci word f, a Fibonacci- (Zeckendorf-) automatic sequence; the property is built from the synchronized factor-equality predicate FactorEq(i,j,n) := forall t<n, f[i+t]=f[j+t] and the novelty predicate Novel(i,n) := forall j<i, not FactorEq(i,j,n). Subword complexity counting is the bounded reduction: p(n) = #{ i : Novel(i,n) and i < (least-occurrence bound) }, which Walnut computes as a synchronized linear representation in Zeckendorf digits.
targets.novelty_justification: Non-textbook because the CONTENT (Fibonacci word is Sturmian, complexity n+1) is classical (first-year-ish), but the DECISION METHOD and the synchronized-automaton certificate for the appearance/recurrence functions are research-grade: producing a finite automaton in Zeckendorf numeration that proves p(n)=n+1 for ALL n simultaneously (an unbounded-forall statement, M2 / box-OUT — no [0,64] sample settles 'for all n') is exactly the Mousavi-Shallit decidability program, not a textbook hand proof. The synchronized recurrence-function bound is genuinely beyond standard course material.
targets.source: C. F. Du, H. Mousavi, L. Schaeffer, J. Shallit, 'Decidability and shortest-period enumeration for Fibonacci-automatic words' (arXiv:1406.0670); J. Shallit, 'The Logical Approach to Automatic Sequences: Exploring Combinatorics on Words with Walnut', Cambridge Univ. Press, 2023, Ch. on Fibonacci numeration.
targets.walnut_predicate:
IN. The Fibonacci word f is the canonical Fibonacci-/Zeckendorf-automatic sequence (Walnut: `F`), and the addition automaton for Zeckendorf numeration is built in, so FO+addition predicates over its digits are decidable. Standard encoding (Mousavi-Schaeffer-Shallit / Shallit's Walnut book):

# factor equality: the length-n factors at i and at j coincide
def feq "?msd_fib At (t<n) => F[i+t]=F[j+t]":
# i begins a "novel" length-n factor: no earlier j gives the same factor
def novel "?msd_fib Aj (j<i) => ~$feq(i,j,n)":

# Subword complexity p(n) = #{ i : novel(i,n) }. The novelty predicate is
# synchronized, so Walnut computes a linear representation for p(n) directly:
#   eval pn n "?msd_fib $novel(i,n)":   (count of i, as a Fibonacci linear rep)
# To DECIDE p(n)=n+1 over all n>=1 without a counting closed form, use the
# Sturmian characterization that complexity is n+1 iff for every n there is
# EXACTLY ONE right-special factor of length n (a factor extendable by both
# 0 and 1). Right-special at position i, length n:
def rspecial "?msd_fib Ej Ek $feq(i,j,n) & $feq(i,k,n) & F[j+n]=@0 & F[k+n]=@1":
# exactly-one right-special factor of each length n (factors compared by feq):
def unique_rs "?msd_fib (Ei $rspecial(i,n)) & (Ai Aj ($rspecial(i,n) & $rspecial(j,n)) => $feq(i,j,n))":
# the load-bearing UNBOUNDED-n sentence (returns TRUE):
eval sturmian "?msd_fib An (n>=1) => $unique_rs(n)":

Walnut synthesizes the automaton for `unique_rs(n)`, then verifies the universal sentence `sturmian` is TRUE over all n. (Equivalently one builds the linear representation of p(n) via `novel` and checks p(n)=n+1 as equality of two synchronized functions; both routes are exact.)
targets.soundness_note: SOUND because it is a decision procedure, not a sample: the Fibonacci word is k-automatic in Zeckendorf numeration (finite-alphabet, finite automaton over Fibonacci digits), addition in that Pisot system is automaton-representable, and the predicates feq/novel/rspecial/unique_rs are all FO over (N, +, <, F[.]). By Buchi-Bruyere the universal sentence `An (n>=1) => unique_rs(n)` is decidable; Walnut synthesizes the corresponding automaton and returns TRUE iff that automaton is universal (its complement is empty), establishing p(n)=n+1 for ALL n, not n<=64. Re-checkable certificate: the emitted automata for feq/novel/rspecial/unique_rs plus the universality/emptiness check on the final automaton are independently re-verifiable by any automaton library; equivalently the linear representation of p(n) and the equality p(n)=n+1 can be re-checked symbolically. box_out=TRUE: a [0,64] bounded box can only confirm p(n)=n+1 for n up to ~64 and cannot discharge the universal forall n>=1 (genuine unbounded-forall, M2 class) — exactly the quantifier Walnut settles. Novelty honestly textbook: p(n)=n+1 is the defining Sturmian property (Morse-Hedlund) and the Fibonacci word is THE canonical worked Fibonacci-automatic Walnut example; true-novelty rating still requires the blind panel.

targets.statement: The Tribonacci word T (fixed point of 0→01, 1→02, 2→0), a Tribonacci-automatic sequence over the Pisot/Tribonacci numeration system (Walnut's msd_trib, with the greedy no-"111" digit condition), is fourth-power-free: it contains squares and cubes but no fourth powers, and its critical exponent equals the known algebraic supremum (≈3.19, real-root value from Mousavi–Schaeffer–Shallit / Tan–Wen). The load-bearing decidable sentence over unbounded period p and shift i is ¬∃i,p (p≥1 ∧ ∀t (t<3p → T[i+t]=T[i+t+p])), decided TRUE by Walnut.
targets.sequence: The Tribonacci word T, a Tribonacci-automatic sequence over the Tribonacci/Pisot numeration system (Walnut's 'msd_trib'). The bounded reduction is the power-occurrence predicate HasPower(i,p,e) := forall t < (e-1)*p, T[i+t]=T[i+t+p], a finite-state predicate on the Tribonacci-digit encodings of i and p; the critical exponent is the supremum of e*p/(e*p) realized, computed as a least/greatest fixpoint over this automaton.
targets.novelty_justification: Research-grade: the exact critical exponent and the complete classification of which powers occur in the Tribonacci word were OPEN problems resolved mechanically by Mousavi-Schaeffer-Shallit; the proofs are not in any textbook and were among the first non-trivial Walnut results in a non-Fibonacci Pisot system. 'No 4th powers for ALL positions/periods' is the genuine unbounded-forall class (box-OUT): a finite [0,64] window cannot certify avoidance over all infinite positions; only the synthesized automaton does.
targets.source: H. Mousavi, L. Schaeffer, J. Shallit, 'Decidability of properties of the Tribonacci word / Mechanical Proofs of Properties of the Tribonacci Word', WORDS 2015, LNCS 9304 (and arXiv:1406.5970); see also J. Shallit, Walnut book (CUP 2023), Tribonacci chapter.
targets.walnut_predicate:
# Tribonacci word T is built into Walnut as automatic seq T over msd_trib.
# 4th-power-free: no (i,p) with p>=1 such that T has period p over a block of length 4p
# (i.e. T[i+t]=T[i+t+p] for all t < 3p).
eval trib4free "?msd_trib ~Ei,p (p>=1 & At (t<3*p) => T[i+t]=T[i+t+p])":
# Equivalently define the power-occurrence predicate and assert no 4th power:
def haspow "?msd_trib At (t+1<=e_minus1*p) => T[i+t]=T[i+t+p]":   # e=4 -> e_minus1*p = 3p
# Walnut returns TRUE. The critical exponent is obtained as the sup of (k+p)/p over the
# maximal-run automaton (def factoreq / least-fixpoint over equality), an exact algebraic value,
# not a sample. 3*p is a constant multiple (p+p+p) -> stays in FO+addition (Presburger), no var*var.
targets.soundness_note: SOUND because Walnut is a complete decision procedure for FO(ℕ,+,<) extended by Tribonacci-automatic predicates (Büchi–Bruyère; Mousavi–Schaeffer–Shallit for the Tribonacci/Pisot system, whose msd_trib addition automaton enforces the greedy no-"111" constraint), not a [0,64] sample. The decision over UNBOUNDED i,p is reduced to an automaton built by product/quantifier-elimination from T's automaton; "fourth-power-free" ⟺ the existential power-automaton recognizes the empty language. Re-checkable CERTIFICATE: the synthesized automaton, whose emptiness (for the ∃ form) or universality (for the ∀ form) is independently verifiable by a standard automaton emptiness/complement check; the critical exponent emerges as the exact algebraic sup of (run+p)/p over the equality/maximal-repetition automaton. box_out=TRUE: fourth-power-freeness quantifies over unbounded periods p and shifts i, so a bounded [0,64] check (i,p≤64) cannot settle it — it is the genuine unbounded-∀/∃ (M2) class, and the empirical 200k-prefix scan (no 4th power; max exponent still climbing ~3.186 at p≈274) is corroboration, not proof.

targets.statement: For the Pell word p (fixed point of 0->001, 1->0; the Ostrowski-alpha word for alpha = sqrt(2)-1) and the optimally constructed Pell-automatic balanced/rich word over Walnut's Pell numeration system msd_pell: (i) no factor has exponent exceeding 2 + sqrt(2)/2, and (ii) factors with exponent approaching 2 + sqrt(2)/2 occur infinitely often. The supremum (critical exponent / repetition threshold for binary rich words) is therefore exactly 2 + sqrt(2)/2, an unbounded sup settled over unbounded n via the Pell-numeration repetition automaton.
targets.sequence: The Pell word / Pell-automatic balanced word (Ostrowski-alpha numeration for alpha = sqrt(2)-1), Walnut's Pell numeration system 'msd_pell'. Bounded reduction: the repetition predicate Rep(i,p,n) over Pell-digit encodings, from which the critical exponent (a quadratic-irrational supremum 2 + sqrt(2)/2) is extracted as a synchronized least-upper-bound over the automaton — note the VALUE is irrational yet still decided, because the relevant FO statement only compares exponents to thresholds, not the irrational itself.
targets.novelty_justification: Strongly research-grade: 2 + sqrt(2)/2 as the repetition threshold for binary rich words was a CONJECTURE of Baranwal-Shallit later resolved; the Pell-numeration adder and the Pell-automaticity of the witnessing word are recent (2019+) research, not textbook. It also showcases the subtle point that an IRRATIONAL critical exponent is still decided by Walnut's FO+addition machinery — far beyond first-year material. Box-OUT: the 'factors approach 2+sqrt(2)/2 infinitely often' clause is a genuine unbounded-exists-for-all-bounds statement.
targets.source: A. R. Baranwal, J. Shallit, 'Critical exponent of infinite balanced words via the Pell number system', WORDS 2019, LNCS 11682, pp. 80-92; A. R. Baranwal, L. Schaeffer, J. Shallit, 'Ostrowski-automatic sequences: theory and applications', Theoret. Comput. Sci. 858 (2021); repetition-threshold-for-rich-words follow-ups (arXiv:1908.03169 / 2406.06513).
targets.walnut_predicate:
Numeration system: msd_pell (Pell/Ostrowski numeration; addition automaton constructed by Baranwal-Shallit / Baranwal-Schaeffer-Shallit and implemented in Walnut). Let P be the Pell-automatic word loaded as a Walnut word automaton. Factor-equality / occurrence over Pell digits:
  def pellfaceq "?msd_pell Ai (i<n) => P[s+i]=P[t+i]":
Repetition (a factor starting at i, period p>0, length n=p+q, exponent (p+q)/p; here parametrize by comparing q to a rational multiple of p to test exponent >= 1 + r):
  def pellrep "?msd_pell p>=1 & q>=1 & (Aj (j<q) => P[i+j]=P[i+p+j])":
Upper bound (no factor exceeds threshold 2+sqrt(2)/2): for every rational r = a/b with a/b > 2+sqrt(2)/2 realizable as a power, assert non-occurrence; the exact lub is the synchronized least-upper-bound over the automaton (Schaeffer-Shallit critical-exponent method). Concretely Currie-Mol-Rampersad's avoidance of 14/5-powers is the Walnut-checkable universal statement:
  eval no_14_5 "?msd_pell ~Ei,p (p>=1 & (Aj (5*j<14*p) => P[i+j]=P[i+p+j]))":   // expect TRUE
Infinitely-often (lower bound, the unbounded ∀∃ / M2 statement):
  eval inf_often "?msd_pell An Ei (i>n) & (factor at i has exponent within 1/k of 2+sqrt(2)/2 for each k)":   // ∀∃ over unbounded n, TRUE
The irrational value 2+sqrt(2)/2 is never represented; only rational p/q thresholds are compared, and the value is extracted as the lub of attained rationals.
targets.soundness_note: SOUND because Walnut runs a decision procedure (Buchi-Bruyere over FO+addition in the Pell/Ostrowski numeration), not a finite sample: for each FO sentence it synthesizes an automaton and returns TRUE iff that automaton is universal (empty complement), which an independent automaton emptiness/universality check re-verifies. The Pell word is k-automatic (finite binary alphabet, Pell-automatic = Ostrowski-alpha for alpha=sqrt(2)-1), and the addition relation in msd_pell is a genuine DFA shipped in Walnut (Baranwal-Shallit; Baranwal-Schaeffer-Shallit), so '+' and indexing P[.] are legal. The critical-exponent claim is decided EXACTLY over unbounded n even though the value 2+sqrt(2)/2 is irrational, because every FO statement only compares factor exponents to RATIONAL thresholds p/q (the repetition predicate pellrep); the irrational sup is recovered as the synchronized least-upper-bound of the attained rationals, never stored. Re-checkable certificate: the emitted automata for (a) the universal upper bound (e.g. TRUE for 'avoids 14/5-powers', the Currie-Mol-Rampersad witness) and (b) the ∀∃ infinitely-often lower bound; both are re-verified by an independent automaton check. This is the genuine unbounded-∀/∀∃ (M2) class: the [0,64] box can exhibit factors but cannot certify that NO factor of ANY length exceeds the threshold nor that approaching factors recur infinitely often, so the box fails to settle it. Literature backing: Baranwal-Shallit, 'Critical exponent of infinite balanced words via the Pell number system' (WORDS 2019, arXiv:1902.00503); Currie-Mol-Rampersad, 'The repetition threshold for binary rich words' (arXiv:1908.03169), proving the threshold is exactly 2+sqrt(2)/2.

targets.statement: Fix a quadratic irrational α (eventually-periodic continued fraction) and work in the Ostrowski-α numeration system. For α, β in the same quadratic field Q(α), the inhomogeneous Beatty sequence B_{α,β}(n)=⌊nα+β⌋ is Ostrowski-SYNCHRONIZED: a finite automaton reading the Ostrowski representations of n and y in parallel accepts iff y=⌊nα+β⌋. Combined with the (automatic) Ostrowski addition relation for quadratic α, the first-order theory of ⟨ℕ,+,<, n↦⌊nα+β⌋⟩ is decidable. Load-bearing target: for α,β with 1/α+1/β=1 (both irrational), the Beatty/Rayleigh complementarity sentence ∀m≥1 ∃!n ( ⌊nα⌋=m ∨ ⌊nβ⌋=m ) — the two homogeneous Beatty sequences partition ℕ — is decided TRUE by Walnut via the Ostrowski adder over UNBOUNDED m.
targets.sequence: The inhomogeneous Beatty sequence B_{alpha,beta}(n)=floor(n*alpha+beta) as an Ostrowski-alpha-synchronized sequence; the bounded reduction is the synchronization automaton Beatty(n,y) := (y = floor(n*alpha+beta)) recognized over paired Ostrowski-numeration digit strings, combined with the Ostrowski addition automaton of Baranwal-Schaeffer-Shallit. Complementarity / three-distance properties become FO+addition sentences over these synchronized predicates.
targets.novelty_justification: Research-grade: while Beatty's theorem itself is classical, the result that the FULL first-order theory with the floor(n*alpha+beta) function is decidable for quadratic-irrational alpha — and mechanizable in Walnut via the Ostrowski adder — is recent (Hieronymi et al. 'Decidability for Sturmian words', CSL 2022; Walnut Beatty-sequence work 2024). This uniform decidability (proving infinitely many Beatty/three-distance identities by one automaton) is firmly beyond textbook scope. Box-OUT: complementarity is forall-m-exists-unique-n over unbounded N, unsettleable by a [0,64] sample.
targets.source: P. Hieronymi, D. Ma, R. Oei, L. Schaeffer, C. Schulz, J. Shallit, 'Decidability for Sturmian words', CSL 2022, LIPIcs 216:24 (arXiv:2102.08207); L. Schaeffer, J. Shallit et al., 'Beatty sequences for a quadratic irrational: decidability and applications' (arXiv:2402.08331); Baranwal-Schaeffer-Shallit, 'Ostrowski-automatic sequences', TCS 858 (2021).
targets.walnut_predicate:
# Numeration: Ostrowski-α for the chosen quadratic irrational α (continued fraction eventually
# periodic), with the Baranwal–Schaeffer–Shallit / Hieronymi–Terry Ostrowski ADDITION automaton
# as the Presburger core. The unbounded-valued Beatty sequence is NOT finite-alphabet, but its
# graph relation is the SYNCHRONIZATION automaton (bounded reduction, exactly the in-scope case):
#
#   Beatty_a(n,y)  := ( y = floor(n*alpha) )        # synchronized, Ostrowski-recognized
#   Beatty_b(n,y)  := ( y = floor(n*beta)  )        # synchronized; for 1/alpha+1/beta=1, beta in Q(alpha)
#
# Membership of m in either Beatty set (existential over the synchronized graph):
#   def inA "?msd_ostrowski_a En (n>=1 & $Beatty_a(n,m))":
#   def inB "?msd_ostrowski_a En (n>=1 & $Beatty_b(n,m))":
#
# Complementarity = covering AND disjointness, over UNBOUNDED m (the genuine forall):
#   eval beatty_partition "?msd_ostrowski_a Am (m>=1) => (($inA(m) | $inB(m)) & ~($inA(m) & $inB(m)))":
#
# (Equivalently the exists-unique form Am>=1 E!n (Beatty_a(n,m)|Beatty_b(n,m)); E! desugars to
# FO+addition.) Walnut returns TRUE and synthesizes the deciding automaton. Three-distance /
# return-word-gap facts are likewise FO+addition over Beatty_a,Beatty_b and decided the same way.
targets.soundness_note: SOUND because it is a decision procedure, not a sample. The Ostrowski-α addition relation for a quadratic irrational is finite-automaton-recognizable (Hieronymi–Terry 2014; Baranwal–Schaeffer–Shallit), and the inhomogeneous Beatty graph y=⌊nα+β⌋ is a SYNCHRONIZED automaton over paired Ostrowski digit strings (Schaeffer–Shallit–Zorcic, arXiv:2402.08331). With + and < and these synchronized predicates, the sentence sits in FO over ⟨ℕ,+,<,Ostrowski-automatic predicates⟩, which is decidable by Büchi–Bruyère; Walnut/Pecan returns TRUE/FALSE AND synthesizes the witness automaton. Critically the complementarity sentence is a TRUE unbounded universal (∀m≥1): the [0,64] box can only confirm finitely many m and cannot settle the ∀ — only the synchronized-automaton decision over unbounded m does (hence box_out=true, the M2 unbounded-∀ class). Re-checkable CERTIFICATE = (i) the Ostrowski adder automaton, (ii) the two Beatty synchronization automata Beatty_a, Beatty_b, and (iii) the synthesized decision automaton, whose TRUE verdict ⟺ universality of the predicate automaton, independently re-verified by an automaton universality / complement-emptiness check. Exact-or-DEFER with a re-checkable witness → SoundFaithfulnessBackend. Caveat: requires the chosen α be a genuine quadratic irrational (eventually-periodic CF) so the Ostrowski adder is finite-state; for non-quadratic α the adder need not be automatic and this would DEFER. Novelty caveat: Beatty/Rayleigh complementarity is a classical textbook theorem (the Walnut method is 2024, but reproving Beatty is the paper's stated 'classical theorem in seconds' demo) — true-novelty calls still need the blind panel, not this probe.

adjudication.best_runnable_candidate: Target 10 — Tribonacci word is fourth-power-free (no factor of exponent ≥ 4), over Walnut's built-in msd_trib Pisot numeration. Single-line eval, no custom automaton to construct, genuinely unbounded ∀i,p (box-OUT), and sits on a research-grade result line (exact critical exponent / power classification of the Tribonacci word, resolved mechanically by Mousavi–Schaeffer–Shallit).
adjudication.best_candidate_walnut_command:
eval trib4free "?msd_trib ~Ei,p (p>=1 & At (t<3*p) => T[i+t]=T[i+t+p])";
# T and msd_trib are built into Walnut; expected verdict TRUE. 3*p = p+p+p stays in FO+addition (no var*var). Walnut emits the synthesized automaton; TRUE ⟺ the existential power-automaton is empty, independently re-checkable by an automaton emptiness check.
adjudication.verdict_paragraph: GREEN. All 12 targets are correctly classified IN-Walnut, and all 12 are genuinely box-OUT: each rests on an unbounded ∀ (or ∀∃) quantifier over n/i/p that a [0,64] sample can at most fail-to-refute, never certify — exactly the M2 escape class the probe is gating for. The adversarial automaticity audit found NO false over-claims of the dangerous kind (asserting a non-automatic sequence is automatic): the canonical-Walnut lane (Thue–Morse, paperfolding, Fibonacci word, Tribonacci) uses sequences that are provably k-/Pisot-automatic and properties that are pure FO+addition (period p always enters additively as offsets, e.g. 3*p = p+p+p, never as a variable product); the m2-sequences lane correctly scopes each unbounded-valued sequence (Motzkin, Apéry, Catalan, Stern) to its bounded automatic reduction (M_n mod 8, a_n mod 8, C_n mod 2^k, the Stern record-setter position indicator) rather than the raw sequence, citing the right tool (Rowland–Yassawi / Krattenthaler–Müller for prime-power moduli, where Christol alone is insufficient). I empirically re-verified the load-bearing arithmetic: Motzkin mod 8 ≠ 0 and v2(M_n) ≤ 2 (n≤4000), Gessel a_n mod 8 = 5^n mod 8 (n≤40), odd-Catalan distinct residues mod 2^k = k−1 (k=2..12), Thue–Morse overlap-free with squares of unbounded period, and Tribonacci 4th-power-free (p≤60) — all hold. The only genuine content defect is Target 4 (Fibonacci recurrence function): its STATED closed form R(n)=F_{k+2}+2F_k−1 is false at every n (I confirmed R(1..8)=3,6,10,11,17,18,19,28 vs claimed 4,8,13,13,22,22,22,36); Walnut would soundly REFUTE that literal identity. This is a content error, not an automaticity over-claim, and it is already self-flagged in the target's own soundness note with the correct form R(n)=n+F_{k+2}−1 — it actually demonstrates the backend's value (a sound REFUTE). Six targets carry honest plausible-non-textbook novelty (paperfolding appearance function, Motzkin mod 8, Gessel/Apéry, odd-Catalan count, Stern record-setters, Pell critical exponent); the rest are honestly textbook. With ≥1 (in fact ≥6) IN-Walnut, box-OUT, plausibly-non-textbook targets each emitting a re-checkable automaton certificate (SoundFaithfulnessBackend, exact-or-DEFER per ADR 0037), the gate passes: build the Walnut backend.
caveats: The hard `novelty` field tags six targets as 'nontextbook' and six as 'textbook'; NONE is literally tagged 'research'. n_research_grade=6 counts the nontextbook ones (the strongest novelty available in this batch). The novelty_justification prose argues 'research-grade' but the structured field downgrades — and the notes are explicit that TRUE novelty needs the blind human panel, not this probe.

Target 4's stated formula is FALSE; Walnut's sound verdict on the literal sentence is REFUTE (counterexample automaton), not TRUE. Keep it as a REFUTE demo, not a confirmation target. Its qualitative class (Fibonacci-piecewise-affine, Θ(n)) is correct.

Targets 5,6,7 require CONSTRUCTING/LOADING a custom DFAO (MOT8 for Motzkin mod 8; APERY8 for a_n mod 8; C mod 2^k) before the eval. These are the genuinely novel headlines but each can DEFER on engineering grounds if the Rowland–Yassawi automaton can't be built/loaded in the given Walnut setup. Do not pick one of these as the FIRST smoke-test.

Targets 2 (RS abelian 2-regularity) and 8 (Stern per-length count floor(3k/4)−(−1)^k) are PARTIAL-DEFERs: the verbatim '2-regular' headline and the count-FUNCTION identity are NOT single FO+addition evals — they are linear-representation / automaton-word-counting facts outside one eval call. Their bounded automatic shadows (Δρ, ρ mod m; the record-setter indicator equality) ARE in scope.
Target 7 is a per-fixed-k SCHEMA — Walnut cannot quantify over k (that ranges over the automaton family). Each k-instance is sound; 'for all k' is not a single decision.

Targets 11 (Pell) and 12 (Beatty) need the msd_pell / Ostrowski-α adder and a loaded word/synchronized automaton; T12 additionally requires α be a genuine quadratic irrational (eventually-periodic CF) or it DEFERs. More setup than the built-in Tribonacci/Thue–Morse cases.

Some predicate bodies contain informal pseudocode (T3 `a = c*?`, T11 `within 1/k of ...`) that is not literal Walnut syntax; these are exposition, not the runnable command. The chosen best command (T10) and the canonical built-ins (T1, T9) are literally runnable as written.

Higher-novelty alternative to run if the backend can load custom automata: Target 5, `eval no8 "?lsd_2 An MOT8[n]!=@0";` (Motzkin numbers never divisible by 8 — a named former conjecture). Chosen T10 instead for the first run because it needs zero custom-automaton construction and is therefore the lowest-risk decisive smoke-test.
